steps.title: Read the passage for the key claim
steps.content: The passage states that 82.9% of Quito respondents use parks versus 72.7% in Lima, yet a much lower percentage of Quito respondents live within a 10-minute walk of a park. The authors conclude: "greater proximity alone can't explain the difference in park use." That sentence is the pivot — it tells you the difference in park use must be caused by something besides proximity.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — It was much larger than the researchers conducting the study expected.: The passage never mentions researcher expectations. ✗
  • Choice B — It is caused by something other than the parks' proximity to city residents.: Directly restates the passage's conclusion that proximity alone cannot explain the difference. ✓
  • Choice C — It could be due to inaccuracies in the survey results.: Survey inaccuracies are never discussed; the passage accepts the data as valid. ✗
  • Choice D — It was calculated using sources that predate the survey.: No predated sources are mentioned anywhere in the passage. ✗
That matches Choice B — It is caused by something other than the parks' proximity to city residents..
